rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR CADIZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/9/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) There is no Exhibit F attached to the petition, showing attorney costs. (2) Paragraph 7 of the prayer includes that $1,533.57 reimbursement of costs to the attorney, but paragraph 9 of the prayer does not reflect that amount in the calculation of the net distribution. (3) Why did the estate pay $1,737.56 for mortgage on 942 Prague on 3/23/16? That was not an asset of this estate. (4) Please explain the $2,125 in attorney fees paid to Errol Zshornack in 2016 and the $3,000 "retainer" paid to Rodel Rodis on 9/20/16. (There was no order or any authority to pay the executor's prior attorney any fees in this estate.) (5) Was there an order authorizing $7,500 to attorney Melissa Polk to be paid out of escrow? (6) Another $9,500 was paid to Mr. Rodis on 7/21/21 for legal fees "re: Iniego lawsuit." Was there an order authorizing that payment? (7) There were several reimbursements to "Cadiz M for estate expenses" in 2019 and 2020 totaling $417.17 without any descriptions or original payees. (8) Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement. (9) For what legal fees and costs were the executor's current attorneys paid $6,675.35 on 5/20/24? (10) The allegations regarding claims against the estate do not appear to be entirely accurate. The register of actions reflects four other claims within the claims period. Only one (Wells Fargo) was withdrawn on 8/23/21. The accounting reflects payments to three of them but not the Nordstom fsb claim for $1,560. No allowance or rejection was filed for any of those claims. (11) Why is the reserve requested so high ($25,000)? The estate has been open for nearly 8 years and the real property was sold in 2019. Please provide a revised proposed order, if applicable, and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/23/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The executor was appointed on 3/21/17 and granted full authority on that date. Errol Zshornack withdrew his RSN on 8/2/19, not 10/18/18. Letters Testamentary were issued on 4/24/17, not 4/22/22. Only the original bank statement verifying the ending balance of an accounting needs to be filed in a decedent's estate unless otherwise requested.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARINA NIKOL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/16/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The instructions for how to appear remotely must be ATTACHED to the notice, not filed separately without any indication of to whom it was sent. (2) Notice was not given to Marta Eisenberg. (3) There were three I&As filed in this case, not just two. (4) Conservator alleges in paragraph 7 of his amended petition that the aggregate value of the estate was $1,486,953.22. The accounting begins with $1,470,549.33. (5) If there were 796.505 shares of Calamos Invt Tr New Mkt Neutral Cl I on hand on 4/18/22 and 11.138 shares were reinvested, why were only 805.643 shares exchanged for 795.579 shares on hand on 4/30/23? (6) Ending balances shown in the account must be reconciled to the statements. [LRSF 14.35 D.] The Wells Fargo checking account statement ending 4/25/23 shows an amount that is less than the amount shown in the accounting. (7) Also no reconciliation is shown for the difference between the Wells Fargo account xxx6790 balance reported in the I&As and the balance shown on the statement for the period ending 4/7/22. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and DIVIDED FROM THE REST OF THE DOCUMENT PAGE BY A PRINTED LINE. [CRC 2.110(a).] Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee WAS appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JEROME GOLDSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/21/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The second amendment was e-filed on 2/6/25. Notice of it mailed on 2/4/25 was defective. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) A person whose right, title, or interest would be affected by a petition filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 (e.g., an heir who is not a beneficiary of the trust) must either receive personal service of notice (of the petition and any supplements or amendments thereto) or must waive notice. [CCP Section 415.10.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/7/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DAVID JOHN SULLIVAN & DIANA WILTSE SULLIVAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/4/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The petition was only cursorily reviewed online, as no courtesy copy was provided. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] It does not appear that all beneficiaries were listed in the petition. [CRC 7.902.] (4) Please file a copy of the vesting deed to show how title to the real property is vested. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/21/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Notice of Remote Appearance is a notification to the court that a party intends to appear remotely, not a notice to interested parties with instructions for how they may appear remotely. Please read the instructions on page 3 of that JC form.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAX G. CRAYTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review counsel's declaration of due diligence and determine whether sufficient efforts have been made to identify and locate relatives of the decedent and his predeceased spouse who would be the decedent's heirs entitled to receive notice and contest the will sought to be admitted to probate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 05/18/2026.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARUKI SETO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/21/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) It does not appear that the will has been FILED in this proceeding. (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedentâs parents and siblings when did they die? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/7/25. A supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, should suffice. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
